author: Niche Gamer
text:
Assassin’s Creed Shadows final update launches this month Publisher Ubisoft and developer Ubisoft Quebec revealed Assassin’s Creed Shadows is getting its final update this month. The game’s final update is launching tomorrow, June 16th, for all platforms and brings with it the new “Black Tides” store quest, to hype up Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, which launches in July. Here’s a rundown on the new update: […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Sonic producer says AAA studios need to learn from indie developers The gap between AAA and indie game development continues to grow, and according to Sonic the Hedgehog producer Takashi Iizuka, that’s not necessarily a good thing. Speaking with GamesRadar+, Iizuka reflected on the realities of modern game development and why major publishers like Sega are increasingly interested in how smaller studios operate. His argument is […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Death Stranding movie won’t retell the game, director confirms If you were expecting the long-awaited Death Stranding movie to simply retell Sam Porter Bridges’ journey, it sounds like that’s not the plan. Director Michael Sarnoski has revealed that the A24 adaptation is being developed as an original story set within the Death Stranding universe rather than a direct recreation of the games. Instead of […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Chris Avellone says Xbox studios are increasingly concerned about layoffs The uncertainty surrounding Xbox’s future appears to be growing, and according to veteran game designer Chris Avellone, even studios that previously felt secure are beginning to worry. Avellone recently commented on the situation inside Microsoft’s gaming division, claiming that several first-party teams are hearing conflicting messages about their future following major leadership changes earlier this […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
New free-to-play Witcher RPG in development, says new rumor A new report suggests that CD Projekt RED could be preparing another trip into the world of The Witcher, but this time with a very different approach. According to the rumor shared by MP1st, an unannounced free-to-play co-op action RPG based on The Witcher is reportedly in development for PC and mobile devices. While the […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Capcom is removing several Dragon’s Dogma 2 DLCs before Dark Arisen arrives Capcom is making some significant changes to Dragon’s Dogma 2 ahead of the launch of its upcoming Dark Arisen expansion. The publisher has confirmed that the Dragon’s Dogma 2 Deluxe Edition and several downloadable content packs will no longer be available for purchase starting June 25, 2026. At the same time, the base game will […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Microsoft CEO says Xbox must become a sustainable business Speaking on the New York Times’ Hard Fork podcast, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella reflected on the company’s long investment in Xbox and suggested that the focus is now shifting toward making the business financially sustainable rather than continuing to heavily subsidize it. According to Nadella, Xbox has delivered tremendous entertainment value over the years, but […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Dwarf Fortress digs up a dinosaur update this month Dwarf Fortress is going prehistoric, with a free dinosaur update that includes exactly 100 new creatures.

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
God of War Laufey reportedly launches in first half of 2027 According to a new rumor, a recent release window for God of War Laufey will launch sometime in the first half of next year.  The new rumor comes from regular leaker NateTheHate, who says his sources point toward a launch during the first half of 2027. Revealed earlier this month, God of War Laufey marks […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Kaiji gets new live-action film in 2027 TOHO has announced another new Kaiji live-action film is coming this year and Tatsuya Fujiwara is returning as the infamous gambler. The new live-action film is titled Kaiji: Life Revenge Game and it will premiere in Japan early next year, on January 29th, 2027. The new Kaiji film is based on the original 2009 manga by […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Ghost in the Shell anime reveals ending theme in latest trailer Bandai Namco Filmworks shared a new trailer for the hotly anticipated The Ghost in the Shell TV anime, revealing the show’s ending theme. The Science SARU-produced TV anime based on the legendary manga of the same name will feature the ending theme “Blue” by MILLENNIUM PARADE with singers Saya Gray and Daniel Caesar. Here’s a new look […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Ninja Scroll 4K remaster coming alongside theatrical re-release Sentai Filmworks has announced a 4K remaster for the iconic 1993 Ninja Scroll anime film alongside a theatrical re-release set for later this year. The 4K remaster (3840 × 2160 pixels) was produced by Sentai under the direct supervision of director Yoshiaki Kawajiri, with the landmark film meticulously scanned from the original 35mm negatives and […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Dokapon 3-2-1 Super Collection! coming to PC this month Japanese developer Sting has announced a PC release for Dokapon 3-2-1 Super Collection!, their new re-release of their classic Dokapon games. The new PC (Steam) release for the games is set for later this month, on June 29th. The release will only support Japanese like its Switch release from earlier this year. As covered in our previous report, the Dokapon […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Star Fox (2026) Preview Daring to dive back into the Lylat System, we're previewing the latest installation of the Star Fox series on Nintendo Switch 2.

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Pocketpair says Palworld 1.0 is its most ambitious release ever It’s easy to forget that despite its enormous success, Palworld is still technically an early access game. That will soon change with version 1.0, and according to Pocketpair, this won’t be just another content patch. The studio is describing it as the biggest and most ambitious update in the game’s history, one that could fundamentally […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Monsters are Coming! gets Xbox release this summer Publisher Raw Fury and developer Ludogram have announced an Xbox release for Monsters are Coming!, their roguelite strategy shooter game. Monsters are Coming! is coming to Xbox Series X|S later this summer on August 6th via the Microsoft Store, as well as Xbox Game Pass. The game has been available for Windows PC (Steam) since November […]

Source
